chunk_summary: This section provides quick reference anchors for outpatient macronutrient decisions in children, including age-specific macronutrient distributions, hydration, and growth management strategies, with a focus on practical application in clinical and programmatic settings.
learning_objectives: Understand age-specific macronutrient distribution ranges and hydration targets for children.
Learn strategies for managing growth faltering and obesity in outpatient pediatric care.
Incorporate programmatic anchors like PM POSHAN meals into nutritional counseling.
Apply WHO and IAP growth standards to assess and monitor pediatric growth.
key_facts: Fibre targets (DRI): 19 g/day (ages 1–3), 25 g/day (ages 4–8), 31 g/day boys and 26 g/day girls (ages 9–13), 38 g/day males and 26 g/day females (ages 14–18).
Hydration (DRI total water AI): 1.3 L/day (ages 1–3), 1.7 L/day (ages 4–8), increasing with heat and activity.
Growth faltering: Aim for weight gain 2–3× the median for age; reassess at 2 weeks and escalate at 4 weeks if no improvement.
Obesity management: Eliminate sugar-sweetened beverages and packaged snacks before reducing home meals; follow up in 4–6 weeks.
PM POSHAN meals: Primary school meals provide ~450 kcal/12 g protein; upper primary meals provide ~700 kcal/20 g protein.
reference_quote: Macronutrient distribution anchor (AMDR): ages 1–3 years fat 30–40% of energy; ages 4–18 years fat 25–35%; carbohydrate 45–65%; protein 5–20% (1–3) and 10–30% (4–18).
document_key_topics: Growth faltering and obesity management in children
Integration of PM POSHAN meals into nutritional plans
